body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

I don't know what he does. >> Oh, he he works in the city lab department of energy and some of their mission has to do with I actually don't know what his real project is. Some of what Nate does has to do with there are irradiators that are used like in a medical setting, >> a big machine that can sterilize blood or sterilize things and it has a radioactive source inside of it >> which is useful for the machine to work but it's also a security risk in the sense take that material from the machine so the US government process within the United Hi, I would like to get started uh as quickly as possible because we only have uh Durk and Roman for the first half an hour. Um Sesh, uh we're actually going to start with you because I think that the conversation around the SVG document will be very quick. Um, so do you have uh so what we're doing right now is we're waiting for a call for community comments to go out. >> Sounds good. >> Uh do you have any >> So I read through the document. I don't have any concerns myself. So I'm not sure what the process is. So um should I send out the community call for comments? >> Uh Cindy can send it out. Um generally what we've been doing is uh kind of composing what that email looks like and then Cindy sends it out. >> Sounds good. um when I sent you the original thing like I don't know a few weeks ago whenever it was there's a couple links in the email that I sent you that are like here's an example from the last time and here's what the process is and all of that. So if if there's anything that you don't have please let us know. >> Sounds good. >> Um do you have >> Hey um Alexis. >> Yeah. >> Can I make a suggestion here? If if and if it's okay with uh Cindy, Cindy, do you think you could take the first shot at least getting the the the the draft message into form for Suresh >> and then Suresh if you then you can customize based on that. I think that would be a good process going forward because most of the time this is going to be very much the same stuff. the you know for for normal documents it's going to go to the same lists unless it fits under the the special category of requiring broader uh uh review >> like I'll look for Cindy. >> Yeah, I mean I I can draft something boilerplate and if you want to add anything to it um yeah that's fine. >> Sounds good. It looks fairly clean to me. Okay. >> Um, sorry. Do any of the other members have any objection to doing that process doing the process that way in the future? >> Sydney start off. >> Yes. >> Cindy stat works Roman and Durk are totally happy with it. Yeah. >> Yeah. And and just to add right what we're aiming I think what we'll aim for then is over time the tooling people can assist us there to to alleviate the work on on Cindy. So I mean I think the longer term arc for me would be you know just this is the first time I'm running through this process first time I'm running kind of through the process is boy it would be really great if we got a little more transparency with the data tracker I know this isn't like balloting in the ISG but just the way that provides you know transparency to everyone following along process that would be really great because less email like workflow through email is is a win >> Robert is in the room >> I think And just a note, we've got observers other than Elliot. So we if people in the room, please use the mics to speak. >> Yes. >> Uh okay. Do we need to figure out anything else for the SVG document? >> Nope. I will draft draft um an announcement, send it to Sesh. If he's good with it, then I will just send it out. >> All right. Great. >> Thank you. Thanks. Point number one, taken care of. All right, so moving on to the 9280 updates. Um, sorry, kind of back to that again a little bit back to the idea of of the tooling. Uh, to me, this is about I mean, it's great that Cindy's doing it, but it should be a template. It should go out like a last call each and every time. I mean, it should be a boiler plate, so we shouldn't have to think so hard about it. >> Yeah, I don't think there's a whole lot of thinking that needs to be done. We already do have the boiler plate. So, >> uh, maybe context. That's yeah. >> So I think the only thing Roman right there might be some context specific to this thing that we might want to add in because it's not something that's gone through the there's no like >> I don't know if there's a way to attach a shepherd right up to it. But if there is then like I I agree with you but if not I think whatever would go into a shepherd write up is probably something I'll add to Cindy's message. I think that's kind of why I thought like it might be more customized. >> Yeah. No, that makes sense. Again, you've been an AD, you know, I've been an AD. It's the idea of you have the last call. there's a boiler plate and you can always modify the last call text for whatever might be bespoke for the document you're about to push out. >> Sounds good. >> Um not in a Yeah, might as well do a told you so moment. So um back in u November 2022 we put to the group a proposal to do this exactly to do all of this kind of stuff that was rejected by the group on the basis of over formalization um despite it being um so that is why we have turnover in the arsam sorry that's why we have turnover in the arsab >> okay um I'm gonna move on to the next item because we only have uh everybody for the next 20 minutes or so. Um okay, so our shepherd is Elliot. I think I'm going to turn this over to you, Elliot, because I'm one of the authors and I feel uncomfortable trying to drive this. >> Okay. Um well, uh first um I want to thank Roman for getting the message to uh the list this morning um in terms of your comments. And uh so where we are in the process just as a reminder is that um the community feedback closed as it says uh on the slide uh in June. Uh what has to happen after that is that we have to sort through what is substantive and what is not. Um RFC 9280 actually doesn't tell us what to do with nonsubstantive comments. like you would say, "Oh, well, they can just be handled by the the authors, which is really what I'm expecting them to do, right? We we the RSAB doesn't really have the right to to do much with nonsubstantive comments. So the all the only thing we can do is say here authors, here here's some things you can think about, but substantive ones, we also don't have much right there either. All we can do is judge, oh, this is substantive." and then we have to turn all the ma the matter back to the RSWG uh for consideration. So what I did and what you guys reviewed was doing that sorting and um so before I go further Roma um if you would just take us through the there were a couple of comments I didn't quite follow in your message and forgive me I literally just saw it just before the meeting. um uh in in particular uh you said that you added some references and I wasn't quite sure I understood the text that you added at the bottom in terms of where it came from and so maybe you could comment. >> Yeah, I I'd be happy to do so. So, thank you for for providing kind of the annotation that you did. I try to enrich it by first just providing the the the mail archive reference to where you pulled your own comments from and where you pulled Mike's comments from. That was my first annotation of your feedback. And then I went through the community call, you know, message by message, uh, and tried to kind of validate everything you already kind of put on the list, which is what how how I came to concurrence with your analysis. But I also observed that it seemed I also observed that there were two other bits of feedback that did not appear to be captured in the list that you made that appear to be substantive to me. Uh and that was what I appended to the end. That was a comment by uh by Eker which was a derived comment from on section I think 76 and then a comment uh much kind of later in the discussion from from Rob. And and that was that was my call out there. I provided a a mail archive reference for for both of those. And I try to snip some part of the of the text that they put on the list uh into that email, but the mail archive reference would be the canonical canonical place. And then on top of my message, I added my own kind of feedback. I I called out one particular block of text that would probably benefit from another look. >> Okay. >> All right. So, thank you for that. The text in 7.6 six that you quote is in response to discussion from the comment that I made. So I think there is a need for clarification to say that that I don't think we need all the text from Eker. The reason is that there were a lot of messages actually not just eers um that responded to that. We just need to say that the R the RSWG is already looking at that and we all await resolution on what where they come for that. Are you okay with that? Well, and again, maybe this is the level of precision we're providing. Uh I I think that certain it the discussion the the feedback that Ekker provided and there was follow-up feedback as well was in response to what you said, but the way I framed the level of specificity you provided, you called out I think section it was se section seven and I took that to mean the introduction in section seven and I think particular >> yeah that wasn't clear to me. So if we generalize it to all of section seven then I'm okay. I just read it as just you know 7.0 more specifically and Eker was was Eker and both Rob were pounding at different portions of I believe section uh section 76. So I didn't I wanted to make sure we didn't lose that. >> Okay. So what I'm going to do if if it's okay with the group is I will make clear that there was discussion going on on the entirety of section 7 uh in the RSWG. Um, and then I'll send this out to the with that w with that understanding if if everybody's okay with that, I'll reformat the message. Alexis, this is where I think you need to come back into play as the chair and just send the message back to the RSWG. You can indicate that I was the shepherd, but I think as chair, you need to manage the process from there. Okay. >> So, actually, could I call a process audible on that? Again, I know this is not a standards working group, but it seems unusual to me for to have the document author running the administrative processes, even if in fact uh they are the chair of that group. So, I think we could support a delegate handling that just to make sure that the conflict of interest is very clean, especially since this document explicitly talks about the conflict of interest of this role. >> Okay. If if that's what people want, as long as there are no buttons to be pushed that I can't push, I'm good with that. Uh, and I mean if we need buttons pushed, we can always have Sandy push the buttons. >> Okay. >> So, but generally speaking, I'm fine with that. Like Alexis does not get involved. So, >> okay. Well, I'll reformat the message. Um, I'll send it out later today so people can see it. And if I don't hear any comments by like Wednesday, I'm just going to forward it off to to the RSWG chairs and to the RSWG for their consideration. Okay. And then we'll expect answers from them at a time period that they decide. And then once they've done that, then we can take the next step. And if there are any concerns, then we can raise them. If there are not any concerns, um then then the document should be considered passed and we'll have to ballot on that. >> Okay. Does anyone have any uh input concerns about that plan? >> Paul, go ahead. >> Um I have a big concern about relitigating in the working group. Uh some of the stuff here, especially the stuff in section 7, is going to open up more. Oh, well, if we still have time to touch this, I want this. And I don't know how like the RSWG can deal with that, but I don't know how RSAB's going to deal with relitigated after it's already been sent back. >> That's that's up to >> I'm sorry. I'm speaking as as document author here. >> Right. As you say, Paul, that's for the RSWG to decide. >> Um >> yeah, it looks like when it comes back to us, >> looks like Pete has some input. Yeah, I I would say that the chairs I mean mostly Russ since he's shephering this document but we will discuss obviously the chairs will I if the RAB said this looks like a sub a substantive comment um we can say discussed by the working group already um here is the result we thought we do not think it needs to be relitigated um back to you guys if you think there is something that really needs to change here. We'll continue discussing it, but we think this is solved. Or we might say this is an issue we will discuss in the working group and and move along. Um and we'll just go through each of the issues that way. Um in in particular though, that does bring me to um and you don't have to. I think we would have no objection if RSAB said by the way we think this is a perfectly good solution to this problem. Um and we can check it with the working group or if ARSAB just wants to say this was an issue that was raised. We don't know whether it's particularly good or not but at least smells substantive to us then we'll deal with it either way RSA wants to go on that. >> Yeah I think we're well down the line at this point though. So I think the best thing Pete is for me to send this to you guys to you guys you you as the chairs can say we already address the issues X Y and Z right >> right and you know if we want to be very uh over the top we can say look at this thread in the RSWG discussion uh this date and here's the pointer and and we consider this closed then RSAB can decide whether or not we got that right. Um but yeah, we we can make that happen if that's what you want to do. >> Um as an author, not as chair, um do you constrain any conversation after that to only the points raised? Right? Like so if you do feel like you need to get input from the working group about something that was raised, do you constrain it to only a conversation about that as opposed to reopening everything? It depends how you deal with the nons substantive comments because if it's a change, it's it's open for discussion. >> We're going to put some constraint on the discussion and say this was the issue that was raised and if people go off into the trees, we're going to say that's off in the trees and has nothing to do with what the RSA asked. But if it generates a change to the document, we're going to ask the RSWG, is this change okay? >> Okay, fair enough. >> Okay. So, yeah, if if everybody's okay with that, then I'll proceed as discussed and then um Pete and Russ and you guys should probably see a message by Wednesday is my guess. >> Great. Okay. We're not doing it this week. >> No, no. You You will have the message. You do it when you want to do it. You deal with it next as you wish. You know, that's that's up to you and the RSWG? >> Paul, >> uh, just checking, Elliot, you are sending it just to the RSWD chairs or to the RSWG? >> Um, I think it should, that's a good question. I think it should go to the RSWD chairs and then you guys can decide. >> Thank you. because of what Russ just said. The last thing I want is relitigating while we're not having adult supervision. >> I I think if in the future we get into the habit of everybody knows that if it comes back from RSAB, then chairs are going to define what the conversation's about. You could just as easily copy it to RSWG and everybody will behave themselves. But since we really haven't gone through this process yet, it's fine if the the um if the parents look at it first before it goes to the children. >> Excellent. Um Elliot, do you have anything else you wanted to cover about this document? >> Nope. >> Does anyone else have anything they wanted to >> Oh, just one one reminder. Um because this document is an update to RFC 9280 the there's a special procedure towards the end where the various groups have to approve specifically ISG and the IAD. So to the chair of the I to the IAB and to the ISG you guys might want to think about your process for that now. >> Sounds good. Acknowledge. Thank you. So, I wanted to unpack that mostly because I provided feedback on that. Uh, it's it's a little confusing to me because the document says specified and we're in a circular loop. Nothing is specified until it's published, but we're supposed to uh we're supposed to ask before publication. >> Um, >> I mean, it's already in the original document. So, >> I appreciate that. I don't know how to properly execute it because IDs don't specify anything. They're just documents in the ether. uh and you know but that's a bar for publication. I again I provide kind of feedback because I think the current language is even worse. >> Okay. What it let's step through that. What it says is updates, amendments, and refinements to this document can be produced using the process described herein, which we're doing, but shall be published and operative only after uh the agreement of the IAB and ISG and ensuring that the LLC has no objections. Those that's the process. >> That that sounds to me like you all should be part of the review of the ID before it gets published. That's what it sounds like to me. It looks like a hole, right? Until we clear it. I think that's how I see it. >> That's right. Yeah, >> that's right. >> So, um, at least from my point of view, I probably prepare like a summary for the IAB, uh, as to what I think the changes are. I can probably circulate it with the RSAB and put it up to vote in the IAB. So, like I don't know like Roman wants to put it up as like a balloting thing or not, but I don't know. Maybe not. >> It would be an ISG decision. So, we would use the decision-making rules. >> No, but would it be a ballot or no? I'd probably run it as an ISG decision. >> Okay, sounds good. The same for the IAB decision >> because we don't have published criteria to use the balloting process for this. >> Okay, cool. >> And now that's it. >> We do a conflict review process and we have balloting procedures for >> You'd have to Yeah, you'd have to make up something different for this, but Okay, got it. >> Yeah. Okay, back to you, Elliot. >> That's it. >> Okay. Uh, does anyone else have anything they want to bring up either around this document? >> So, for the IF stream since Durk is on the IAB, so I think we are covered for that even though it's not specifically stated like he's also a stream manager, right? >> Yes. >> So, we'll cover it in the IAB decision. >> Okay. >> Do we have any other pieces of business to bring up? No. All right, >> Elliot, come on. You need to say, "Oh, I forgot. >> I I forgot I have to eat lunch." See you. >> Cool. Our SAB meeting concluded. Thank you everybody. >> Thanks everyone. Thanks.
